notes: No state sales tax. Localities may impose up to ~7%; out of scope for this dataset.
Ala. Admin. Code r. 810-6-1-.84: repair labor exempt only when separately stated from parts; if not itemized, entire charge taxable. Parts always taxable.

Ark. Code 26-52-301; 006.05.08 Ark. Code R. 9: 'initial installation, alteration, addition, cleaning, refinishing, replacement and repair of motor vehicles' is a taxable service. Both parts and labor taxable.
Arizona TPT Ruling TPR 93-31: separately stated repair labor on tangible personal property is not taxable; parts taxable as retail. Lump-sum makes whole charge taxable.

Cal. Rev. & Tax. Code §6012; CDTFA Reg. 1546 / Pub. 25: separately stated repair labor on motor vehicles not taxable; parts taxable. Fabrication labor is taxable. Hazardous-waste/EPA disposal fees generally taxable when associated with taxable parts sale.
Colo. Code Regs. §201-4 'Special Sales/Use Tax Regulations': separately stated labor/service charges on auto repair are deductible from taxable amount; parts taxable.

Conn. Gen. Stat. §12-407(a)(37)(EE); CT DRS PS 92-8: motor vehicle repair services (parts AND labor) fully taxable. CT is one of the few states that taxes all motor vehicle repair labor regardless of itemization.

9 DCMR §463; DC Code §47-2001(n)(1)(N): charges for labor or materials to repair, alter, mend, or fit tangible personal property are taxable regardless of whether labor is separately stated. Diagnostic/wash/wax/towing exempt.
No state sales tax (gross-receipts tax on businesses, not sales tax on customer invoices). Out of scope.
Fla. Stat. §212.05; FDOR GT-800010: when a repairer supplies any parts/materials, the total amount charged for repair (parts + labor) is taxable. Labor-only repairs (no parts) not taxable.

Ga. Comp. R. & Regs. 560-12-2-.78 and 560-12-2-.09: separately stated repair/installation labor on motor vehicles not taxable; parts taxable. Fabrication labor taxable. Lump-sum invoices fully taxable.
Haw. Rev. Stat. §237 (General Excise Tax): 4% GET applies to gross receipts of repair businesses (parts + labor). Not a true sales tax but functions as one to consumer.

Iowa Code §423.2(6); Iowa DOR Taxable Services list: vehicle repair, including engine, body, accessories — both parts and labor taxable. If separately itemized, only materials taxed; if not itemized, full charge taxed.
Idaho Code §63-3613; ISTC Repair Shops guide: separately stated repair labor on tangible personal property (incl. vehicles) not taxable; parts taxable. Combined invoice = entire amount taxable.

35 ILCS 105/3-10; IDOR FAQ: labor charges separately contracted from selling price of tangible personal property are not taxable; parts taxable as retail. Lump-sum makes labor part of taxable selling price.

Ind. Code §6-2.5-1-5; IDOR Sales Tax Information Bulletin #2: separately stated installation/labor charges not in 'gross retail income'; parts taxable. Unitary-transaction rule applies if not itemized.

Kan. Stat. Ann. §79-3603(p),(q); KDOR Pub. KS-1526: services of 'repairing, servicing, altering or maintaining' motor vehicles are taxable. Labor on motor-vehicle repair performed in Kansas is fully taxable; both parts and labor.

Ky. Rev. Stat. §139.200 (as amended HB 487, effective 7/1/2018): when taxable repair parts are sold, the associated installation/labor charges are also subject to tax even if separately stated. Labor-only with no parts is not taxable.
La. Rev. Stat. §47:301(14)(g): labor to fabricate or repair movable property (tangible personal property, including motor vehicles) is taxable. Both parts and labor taxable.
830 CMR 64H.1.1 (Service Enterprises): separately stated repair labor on tangible personal property not taxable; parts taxable. If parts >=10% of total and not separately stated, full amount taxable.
Md. Code Tax-Gen §11-101(l)(3); Comptroller Business Tax Tip #7: labor for repairing tangible personal property (restoring used property) is exempt; parts taxable. Fabrication labor taxable.
36 M.R.S. §1752; MRS Auto Repair Bulletin: separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable. Lump-sum invoices fully taxable.
Mich. Comp. Laws §205.51; Mich. Treas. RAB: separately stated repair labor on tangible personal property not taxable; parts taxable as retail. Lump-sum charges fully taxable.

Minn. Stat. §297A.61, subd. 3; MN DOR Motor Vehicle Repair Industry Guide: separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable. Painting labor and accessory-installation labor taxable. Shop fees taxable.
Mo. Rev. Stat. §144.020; MO DOR Automotive Repair Tax Matrix: separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable. Fabrication labor taxable.
Miss. Code §27-65-23: 'repairs of any kind of tangible personal property' taxable at 7%; both parts and labor taxable on motor vehicle repair.
No state sales tax. Out of scope.

N.C. Gen. Stat. §105-164.4(a)(16); NCDOR RMI guidance: gross receipts from repair, maintenance & installation (RMI) services on tangible personal property — including motor vehicles — taxable. Labor and parts both taxable; installation charges taxable even when separately stated.
N.D. Cent. Code §57-39.2-04; ND OST Service Stations & Garages Guideline: separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable. Lump-sum invoice fully taxable.

Neb. Rev. Stat. §77-2701.16; Reg-1-082; NE DOR Motor Vehicle Services Guide 6-540: separately stated repair labor on motor vehicles not taxable; parts taxable. Painting labor and accessory-install labor taxable; production labor taxable. Shop fees follow taxable charge.
No state sales tax. Out of scope.

N.J. Rev. Stat. §54:32B-3(b); NJ Div. of Tax. ANJ-6: 'maintaining, servicing or repairing' tangible personal property (incl. motor vehicles) taxable. Both parts and labor taxable on motor vehicle repair. Some installation labor on used parts not taxable when separately stated.
N.M. Stat. §7-9-3.5 (Gross Receipts Tax): GRT applies to receipts from performing services in NM — motor vehicle repair labor and parts both taxable.
Nev. Rev. Stat. §372.025; NDOT Repairs and Reconditioning guide: separately stated repair/installation labor not taxable; parts taxable. Bundled charges fully taxable.

N.Y. Tax Law §1105(c)(3): 'maintaining, servicing or repairing' tangible personal property is a taxable service. NY State Pub. TG Auto Repair Bulletin: total charge for parts + labor on motor vehicle repair is taxable. Towing and storage taxable.

Ohio Rev. Code §5739.01(B); ODT guidance: motor vehicles are tangible personal property and repair labor on them is taxable when not separately stated. Separately stated repair labor charges are exempt from sales tax.

Okla. Stat. §68-1354; Okla. Admin. Code 710:65-19-11: separately stated labor/installation charges not taxable; parts taxable. Lump-sum charges: shop pays sales tax on parts at purchase, no tax to customer.
No state sales tax. Out of scope.

72 Pa. Stat. §7201(k)(4); 61 Pa. Code §31.5, §31.50: 'inspecting, altering, cleaning, lubricating, polishing, repairing or waxing motor vehicles' is a taxable service. Both parts and labor on motor vehicle repair are fully taxable.
R.I. Gen. Laws §44-18-12; 280-RICR-20-70-28: separately stated repair labor and installation labor not taxable; parts taxable.
S.C. Code §12-36-910(B); SC DOR Reg 117-306 (Repairs): separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable as retail sale. Lump-sum charges fully taxable.
S.D. Codified Laws §10-45-4; SD DOR Motor Vehicle Repair Tax Fact: auto repair, body repair, oil changes — full charge (parts + labor + delivery) taxable.

Tenn. Code §67-6-205(c)(1); Tenn. Comp. R. 1320-05-01-.54; TN DOR SUT-22: charges for repair services to tangible personal property (motor vehicles) — including all parts, labor, service calls, mileage — are taxable.

Tex. Tax Code §151.0048; 34 Tex. Admin. Code §3.290: motor vehicle repair labor is NOT taxable (motor vehicle repair is excluded from taxable insurance/repair services). Parts are taxable. Shop supplies / EPA / hazmat fees follow the taxable parts charge per Comptroller Pub. 94-113.

Utah Code §59-12-103(1)(g); USTC Pub. 5: charges for labor to repair, renovate, or clean tangible personal property are taxable. Both parts and labor on motor vehicle repair are fully taxable. Note: farm tractor/equipment repair is exempt.

Va. Code §58.1-609.5(2); §58.1-609.5(8) (eff. 7/1/2023): separately stated repair labor charges exempt; parts taxable. Diagnostic labor explicitly exempt when separately stated. Separately stated shop supply charges are taxable.
32 V.S.A. §9701(7); VT Dept. of Taxes GB-1161 (Auto Repairs Industry Guide): separately stated repair labor not taxable; parts taxable. Combined parts/labor charge fully taxable.
Wash. Rev. Code §82.04.050; WA DOR Auto Dealers Guide (Repairs): retail sales of repair services to consumers — labor + parts — fully taxable as 'retail sale' (B&O Retailing classification).

Wis. Stat. §77.52(2)(a)10; Wis. Admin. Code Tax 11.83: labor for repair, service, alteration, fitting, cleaning, painting, coating, inspection, and maintenance of a motor vehicle is taxable. Both parts and labor taxable.
W. Va. Code §11-15-3; §11-15-8: services taxable by default unless specifically exempt. Auto repair labor and parts both taxable.

Wyo. Stat. §39-15-103(a)(i)(J): sales tax applies to services for the repair, alteration, or improvement of tangible personal property. Both parts and labor on motor vehicle repair are taxable. State-owned vehicle exception under §39-15-105.
